page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m8.3xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with 12 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor operating at a base frequency of 2.5 GHz. The instance features a high resource density configuration with 96.0 GB of system memory, establishing a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. It does not include any local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core execution, such as stress-ng single-core CPU tests and single-threaded gzip compression, falls into the bottom 25% tier. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, as demonstrated by the middle 50% rankings in stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and bzip3 decompression tests. Memory bandwidth also performs in the average range, reaching 72.4 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 45.95 GB/sec for write operations.

This hardware profile is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads that do not demand extreme single-threaded CPU performance. The 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it an efficient choice for database operations, caching layers like Redis (which achieved 10.27 million operations per second in testing), and moderate web serving. Conversely, it is less suitable for heavy single-threaded processing or large-scale LLM prompt processing, where its Intel Xeon processor scored in the bottom 10% for medium-sized 7B models.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m8.3xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 12 dedicated Intel Xeon physical cores running at 2.5 GHz on an x86_64 architecture. Equipped with 96.0 GB of RAM, it delivers a high 8.0 GB per core memory density, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Network throughput is anchored at a 4 Gbps baseline. Benchmark data reveals a contrast between weak single-core performance and average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. The instance excels in memory-bound tasks, showing solid results in Redis operations (10.27 million operations/sec) and database workloads, while proving less efficient for compute-heavy or single-threaded applications. It is best suited for caching layers, databases, and general memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m8.3xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 12 Intel Xeon cores, 96 GB RAM, and 4 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering average multi-core performance for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m8.3xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 12 Intel Xeon cores, 96 GB RAM, and a 4 Gbps baseline network speed.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio, tailored for database operations, caching, and multi-core enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 12 dedicated Intel Xeon physical cores running at 2.5 GHz
96.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization
Average multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Well-suited for Redis caching, databases, and memory-bound applications
Lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware
